# HPC Overview {#sec-hpc-overview}

## What we have

Our lab has been allocated significant computational resources on Berkeley's Savio HPC cluster:

### Storage

| Tier | Capacity | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| Home directory | 30 GB per user | Backed up regularly |
| Group directory | 200 GB shared | Shared tools and configs |
| Project directory | 112 TB | Primary lab data store |
| Scratch | 9 PB (shared across campus) | **Not backed up** — purged after 60–90 days of inactivity |

### Compute

| Resource | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Nodes | 4 |
| CPUs per node | 56 |
| RAM per node | 512 GB |
| **Total** | **224 CPUs, 2 TB RAM** |

## Glossary

### Common acronyms

| Acronym | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| BRC | Berkeley Research Computing — the team who manages the HPC resources |
| HPC | High Performance Computing |
| CPU | Central Processing Unit |
| GPU | Graphics Processing Unit |
| RAM | Random Access Memory |
| SSH | Secure Shell — protocol for remote access to Savio |
| SLURM | Simple Linux Utility for Resource Management — the job scheduler |
| QoS | Quality of Service — the queues available for different partitions |

### HPC terms

**Savio**
: Berkeley's primary HPC system — a Linux cluster with 600+ nodes and 15,000+ processors.

**Condo**
: PIs can purchase hardware to contribute to the HPC, gaining priority access equivalent to the resources purchased.

**Core**
: One processing unit within a node (often used interchangeably with CPU in HPC contexts).

**Node**
: A single computer within the cluster, containing CPUs, memory, and local storage.

**Partition**
: A collection of nodes with similar properties or access policies.

**Job**
: A computational task submitted to the SLURM scheduler with specific resource requirements.

**Walltime**
: The maximum real-world time allowed for a job before the scheduler terminates it.

**Open OnDemand**
: A web-based interface for interactively using HPC resources.

## Getting started checklist

New lab members should complete the following before beginning work on Savio (this overlaps with the general onboarding in @sec-onboarding):

- [ ] Review the [Savio documentation](https://docs-research-it.berkeley.edu/services/high-performance-computing/overview/)
- [ ] Obtain a Savio account and join both lab projects (`fc_carleton2025` and `co_carleton`)
- [ ] Set up two-factor authentication and configure your connection
- [ ] Create your personal directory structure
- [ ] Install [Globus Connect](https://www.globus.org/) for file transfers
- [ ] Get Dropbox access as needed and learn to sync to/from Savio via Globus
- [ ] Load and test standard lab environments (see @sec-containers)
- [ ] Run a test job to verify access
- [ ] Review the lab data registry
- [ ] Add your contact info to the lab HPC user list

### Requesting a BRC account

There are two projects you must request access to:

1. `fc_carleton2025`
2. `co_carleton`

Request access through the [MyBRC portal](https://mybrc.brc.berkeley.edu/).
